frames: The agent stands upright, with its arms bent at the elbows and hands held near its chest.
The agent begins to lean its torso forward, bringing its arms closer to its body.
The agent continues to lean forward, extending its arms slightly outwards and downwards.
The agent's torso is now significantly bent forward, with its arms extended further downwards and backward.
The agent's body is leaning far forward, almost parallel to the ground, with its arms extended backward.
The agent's upper body is nearly horizontal, and its legs remain straight, maintaining contact with the ground.
The agent's entire body is tilting forward, with its head and torso very low, and its legs are starting to lift slightly.
The agent has fallen completely forward, with its upper body and head making contact with the ground, and its legs are now off the ground.
